query: Florida First District Court of Appeal redistricting 2026 congressional map emergency petition

key_facts: The Florida First District Court of Appeal (1st DCA), based in Tallahassee, has appellate jurisdiction over Leon County Circuit Court, where most Florida redistricting cases are filed.
Florida's 2022 congressional map (SB 2-C) was drawn at Governor DeSantis's direction and dismantled the majority-Black 5th Congressional District.
Multiple legal challenges to SB 2-C were filed alleging violations of Florida's Fair Districts constitutional amendments (Article III, Sections 20-21).
The 1st DCA previously stayed a lower court ruling against the map before the 2022 elections, allowing SB 2-C to be used.
Litigation over SB 2-C was ongoing as of mid-2025, with a trial on the merits held or expected in Leon County Circuit Court.
Emergency petitions at the 1st DCA would be the expected procedural mechanism if a trial court ordered a new congressional map ahead of the 2026 elections.
Florida's candidate qualifying periods and primary election dates create hard deadlines that drive emergency redistricting litigation.
IMPORTANT LIMITATION: All web search and fetch tools were unavailable during this session. Specific 2026 developments could not be verified.

data_points.metric: Year Fair Districts amendments adopted by Florida voters
